Nearly 1000 teachers of north
east Washington gathered in Spo-
kane today to attend the joint in
stitute to be held today and to-
morrow by Spokane, Stevens and
Lincoln counties and Spokane
city. At the close of the institute
at, noon the teachers will remain
to participate in the meetings of
the Washington State Teachers as
ISOLATION, which convenes tomor-
row afternoon for two days ses-
sion.


County Superintendent McFar-
land called the joint institute to
order at $3.50 this morning at the
South Central high school and
made an address of welcome on
behalf of the four superintendent.
He spoke of Spokane as the great
convention city of the state and
felt that this city would give a
proper account of itself in the en-
tertainment of the teachers this
week.


He then yielded the gavel to
City Superintendent B. M. Watson,
who presided during the balance
of the morning session. Miss Mar
garet Craig, superintendent of Lin
= coin county, was in the chair this
afternoon. The program was car
Yied out largely as previously out
lined


There will be no session tonight.
1t is probable that tomorrow's SEES
sion of the state association will
be held at the First NJ E. church
